---
title: Overview of the BIOMASS R package \vspace{-3mm}\includegraphics[width=1.8cm]{img/logo.png}
author: "D. Lamonica (IRD - AMAP)"
institute: "GEO-TREES Panama Workshop"
date: "March 15, 2026 "
format: 
  beamer:
    slide-level: 2
    theme: default
    colortheme: seagull
header-includes: 
- \titlegraphic{\hspace{-10mm}\includegraphics[width=5cm]{img/logo_esa.jpg}\hspace{-5mm}\includegraphics[width=3cm]{img/logo_geotrees.png}\hspace{4mm}\includegraphics[width=3cm]{img/logo_IRD.png}}
- \setbeamertemplate{section page}{\centering\begin{beamercolorbox}[sep=12pt,center,colsep=-4bp,rounded=true,shadow=true]{part title}\usebeamerfont{section title}\insertsection\par\end{beamercolorbox}}
- \setbeamertemplate{footline}[page number]
---

# Brief introduction

## For what ?

**Estimate Aboveground Biomass and Its Uncertainty in Tropical Forests**

$\rightarrow$ contains functions for estimating above-ground biomass/carbon and its uncertainty in tropical forests. These functions allow to:

1. correct taxonomy (typos & synonyms),
2. estimate wood density and its uncertainty,
3. build height-diameter models,
4. manage tree and plot coordinates,
5. estimate above-ground biomass/carbon at stand level with associated uncertainty,
6. predict and map biomass from LiDAR data with associated uncertainty.

## CRAN R package \hspace{1mm} \includegraphics[width=1.3cm]{img/R_logo.svg.png}

**Current CRAN version: 2.2.7**  \newline 
\vspace{-0.2cm}
![](img/screen_cran_227.png){height=100%}
\newline

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Archives** \newline
\vspace{-0.2cm}
![](img/screen_archive.png){height=30%} 
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
\vspace{1.1cm}
Almost 10 years of existence!
:::
::::

## GitHub public repository

![](img/package_repo.png){height=100%}
\newline

- Entire code accessible, issues open
- Developing version: 3.0
- Continuous integration

## Vignettes

![](img/vignettes.png){height=100%}
\newline

- Tutorial for the entire pipeline divided in three main articles, applied on reduced Nouragues data (French Guiana)

## 
![](img/workflow_BIOMASS_v8_noshiny.png){height=100%}

## 
![](img/workflow_BIOMASS_v8_shiny.png){height=100%}

## Shiny app - BIOMASSapp \hspace{1mm} \includegraphics[width=1.3cm]{img/logo_shiny.jpeg}


- Estimate AGBD per subplot with uncertainties, and spatialize stand metrics

- No map prediction: target users & technical limitations
\vspace{0.4cm}

  $\rightarrow$ **Why such a tool ?**

- Need for a graphical, user-friendly interface for non coding and/or fieldwork people...

- ... actually even R people are using it! It helps for data checks, or visualization for example.

- Issues or feedback to be reported on the GitHub repository


# BIOMASSapp demonstration

# Predict AGBD maps using inventory and LiDAR data

## AGBD-LiDAR modelisation: proposed statistical framework

- geostatistical model with SPV-I/C (SPatially Varying Intercept/Coefficients) to integrate spatial correlation:

- $y(s) = (\alpha + \tilde{\alpha}(s)) + (\beta + \tilde{\beta}(s)) \times x(s) + \epsilon(s)$
\newline
with $\tilde{\alpha}(s_1),...,\tilde{\alpha}(s_n) \sim MVN(0,C_{\alpha}(s_i,s_j))$

- references
![](img/babcock.png){width=50%}![](img/hunka.png){width=50%}


## Implementation in BIOMASS

- inference and predictions done using brms R package ![](img/brms.png){height=15%} 

- two functions: 1st calibrates the model with user plot and LiDAR data, 2nd predicts AGBD and its associated uncertainties over the full raster footprint provided by the user

- full continuity of the pipeline: the inputs of those functions are the outputs from the previous steps

- propagate all AGBD uncertainties from the previous steps, within Bayesian framework

## Example - Nouragues: input data
 ![](img/inventory_tree.png){height=35%}\hspace{0.2cm}![](img/Nouragues_CHM_1m.png){height=40%} \newline

- Tree inventory & (including coordinates)
- LiDAR metric landscape level (e.g. CHM)

$\rightarrow$ BIOMASS pipeline to run...

## ... to get predicted variable $y$ & predictive variable $x$

![](img/data_agbd.pdf){height=52%} ![](img/incertitudes.png){height=15%}\hspace{0.1cm} $\sim$ \hspace{0.1cm} ![](img/data_chm.pdf){height=52%} \newline

\vspace{0.2cm}
- $y$ AGBD 0.25ha for each (sub)plot with uncertainties 
- $x$ mean CHM 0.25ha for each (sub)plot
- distances between plots


## CHM-AGBD model calibration

The function returns a *brms.fit* object \newline

![](img/summary_fit.png){height=50%}\hspace{0.2cm}![](img/ppc.png){height=37%} \newline

$\rightarrow$ inference results to be checked


## Map predictions: arguments

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="40%"}
![](img/chm_arg.pdf){height=100%} 
:::

::: {.column width="60%"}

\vspace{2cm}

- LiDAR metrics at landscape level, resolution managed inside the function

- *brms.fit* object, output of calibration step

- optional: another raster for footprint
:::

::::


## Map predictions: output
![](img/fitted_data_brms_v2.pdf){height=100%}

## Validation

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](img/vignette_validation.png){height=90%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

\vspace{2cm}

- for now, no embedded validation procedure in the package

- in the vignette we propose a example of validation method

- to be discussed this week?
:::

::::

# Code for BIOMASS pipeline

## 5118 lines of R code in the entire BIOMASS package
![](img/cloc_biomass.png){height=100%}
\newline


## Yet...
![](img/screen_full_pipeline.png){height=100%}
\newline

... the full pipeline can be less than 60 lines, comments included!

# Future work, this week and later on 

##

- More robust quality check with single or multiple dates

- Implementation of one or more new approaches for estimating height in the absence of field data

- Taking into account positioning uncertainty on individual trees

- Management of circular plots in the spatialisation pipeline for estimates

- Flexibility (functional form, predictive metric) of the AGBD-LiDAR model (implemented in v3.0)

- Temporal BIOMASS (multiple census and LiDAR acquisitions)


## Authors (v2-v3) and funders
\vspace{.5cm}
Dominique Lamonica, Arthur Bailly, Jérome Chave, Guillaume Cornu, John Godlee, Fabian Fischer, 
Arthur Péré & Maxime Réjou-Méchain
\vspace{.7cm}

![](img/logo_esa.png){height=10%} European Spatial Agency \newline

![](img/logo_IRD.png){height=15%} Research Institute for Development \newline

![](img/logo-cirad.png){height=12%} the French agricultural research and cooperation organization
